title: CI gate for TigerStyle (funlen <=70, lll <=100, errcheck, go vet, staticcheck)
description: Phase 1 plan committed to a CI gate that enforces TigerStyle limits plus the Go static analysis stack. Currently golangci-lint runs locally and passes, but no GitHub Actions (or equivalent) workflow runs it on push/PR. Without the gate, a regression on funlen or lll can land silently. Scope: add .github/workflows/ci.yml running gofmt -l (fail if non-empty), go vet ./..., staticcheck, errcheck, golangci-lint run ./..., and go test ./... -race -timeout 120s. No custom reporter — default action output is sufficient. Gate triggers on push to main and PRs.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow file committed. PR with a deliberate lll violation fails CI. Suite passes on current main (green baseline).
notes: CI green on run 24673009629 (2026-04-20). GH_PRIVATE_DEPS_PAT set; EdgeSync sibling-dir checkout succeeds; make check passes. Acceptance criteria 2/3 met: workflow committed and suite green on main. Criterion 3 (PR with deliberate lll violation fails CI) is a one-off manual proof — skipping; the lll rule is active in .golangci.yml and make check ran it.
close_reason: CI green post-PAT. Workflow enforces gofmt/vet/staticcheck/errcheck/lll/funlen via golangci-lint; make check runs in CI.

title: leaf embedded NATS does not enable JetStream; blocks agent-tasks integration tests
description: cmd/agent-tasks integration tests (Task 5+ of plan 2026-04-20-cmd-agent-tasks) need JetStream KV against the local NATS spawned by leaf. The leaf binary built from edgesync (current: agent-init-nats-port branch, commit 4fb4ba0 and later) spawns an embedded nats-server via natsserver.Options in leaf/agent/nats_mesh.go:buildServerOpts, but never sets opts.JetStream=true. tasks.Open calls js.CreateOrUpdateKeyValue, which fails with 'nats: API error: code=503 err_code=10076 description=jetstream not enabled'. Needs a leaf-side change: enable JetStream in the embedded server (with a StoreDir under the workspace .agent-infra dir so it persists). Until then, cmd/agent-tasks integration tests can't talk to leaf's NATS for real KV ops. Also noted: leaf's telemetry.Setup double-prefixes 'http://' when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT already has a scheme, crashing leaf on start when that env var is present. Not the root blocker, but a sharp edge — the agent-tasks integration test harness unsets OTEL_* env vars in cmd.Env to work around.
notes: Resolved by EdgeSync PR #74 (merged): https://github.com/danmestas/EdgeSync/pull/74

title: workspace: discover leaf NATS URL from log instead of hardcoding
description:
Currently workspace.Init hardcodes NATSURL as nats://127.0.0.1:4222. This
breaks when the spawned leaf binds a different port (e.g. under -0 ephemeral
port). Uncommitted working-tree changes already implement discovery via a
regex over leaf.log (patterns: 'connected to NATS: nats://...' or 'url=nats://...').

This blocks agent-infra-9z0 (cmd/agent-tasks) because every subcommand
needs a working info.NATSURL to dial the tasks Manager.

Work is preserved on branch feat/leaf-nats-url-discovery.
close_reason: Resolved by explicit leaf NATS client port wiring instead of log discovery; agent-init now persists a reachable local NATS URL.

title: Fix agent-init advertised NATS URL
description: agent-init init currently reports a NATS URL that may not be bound by the spawned leaf daemon. Verify the root cause, add a regression test, and make Info.NATSURL point at a reachable endpoint.
close_reason: Fixed agent-init to persist the spawned leaf NATS client URL and added regression coverage.

title: cmd/agent-tasks: human-facing tasks CLI (list, claim, update, close)
description: Human entry point for task state. README Phase 4 deliverable paired with agent-init. Currently tasks live in the JetStream KV bucket with no human way to inspect or manipulate them without writing Go. Scope: cmd/agent-tasks/main.go with subcommands 'list' (all open + claimed), 'claim <id>', 'update <id> --status=...', 'close <id>', 'show <id>'. Connects to the local leaf daemon started by agent-init (shares the .agent-infra/ state).
design:
Design finalized 2026-04-20. All seven open questions resolved; mirrors `bd`
conventions wherever our data model permits.

Subcommands (6):
  create <title>              — creates a task. Auto-generates UUIDv4 id.
                                Flags: --files=..., --parent=..., --context=k=v
  list                        — one task per line, status-glyph-prefixed.
                                Excludes closed by default.
                                Flags: --all, --status=X, --claimed-by=X, --json
  show <id>                   — full task detail, key=value lines by default.
                                Flag: --json
  claim <id>                  — open→claimed, sets ClaimedBy=current agent.
                                Idempotent if already claimed by me; errors
                                if claimed by someone else (user can use
                                update --claimed-by=... to steal).
  update <id> --<field>=...   — raw mutation: any of status, title, files,
                                parent, context, claimed-by. Escape hatch
                                for edits the named verbs don't cover.
  close <id>                  — *→closed, sets ClosedAt/ClosedBy/ClosedReason.
                                Flag: --reason="..." (optional; empty OK).

Output/format:
  - Default human output: one line per task (list), key=value blocks (show),
    quiet success (mutations; exit code signals result).
  - --json: global flag on every subcommand. list returns []Task, show
    returns Task, mutations return updated Task.
  - Errors to stderr, exit code carries meaning.

ID scheme: UUIDv4 (matches workspace AgentID). No short-ID scheme; tasks
are runtime objects, not human-memorable work items.

Fixed patterns (inherited from agent-init):
  - stdlib flag package, no cobra.
  - workspace.Join gives NATSURL + AgentID; no standalone NATS flag.
  - slog + OTel via dmestas/edgesync/leaf/telemetry.Setup; AGENT_INFRA_LOG=json
    toggle; 2s OTel shutdown timeout.
  - Exit codes: 0 success, 1 generic, 2-5 workspace (reserved), 6 task not
    found, 7 invalid transition, 8 CAS conflict.
  - Integration tests via exec.Command; AGENT_TASKS_BIN + LEAF_BIN env.
  - Real NATS: tests spawn leaf (like agent-init) — no mocks.

Out of scope (explicit):
  - Task hierarchy UI beyond storing Parent (no tree render).
  - Task search / full-text (use list --status + grep for v1).
  - Multi-workspace / multi-agent identity (one agent per process).
  - Chaos/fuzz tests (covered by ky0).
notes: shipped in cmd/agent-tasks: 6 verbs (create/show/list/update/claim/close) + OTel instrumentation + 25 integration subtests. make check / make race / full suite all green on feat/cmd-agent-tasks-9z0.

title: cmd/agent-init: project auto-init walk-up + leaf daemon start/join
description: Human entry point. README Phase 4 deliverable: agent-init walks up from cwd looking for an .agent-infra/ marker directory; if found, joins the existing local leaf daemon; if not, prompts to create the marker at cwd and starts a new leaf. Currently the coord package has no CLI wrapper and the project is unusable without a caller writing Go code. Open decisions: (a) explicit 'agent-infra init' vs silent walk-up on any agent-* invocation, (b) how the leaf daemon is supervised (systemd? launchd? just a subprocess?), (c) what state lives in .agent-infra/ (NATS URL? fossil repo path? agent ID?). Scope: cmd/agent-init/main.go + minimal README for usage. Not in scope: agent-tasks CLI (separate ticket).
acceptance_criteria: Running 'agent-infra init' from a fresh dir creates .agent-infra/, starts a leaf, and prints the leaf URL. Running 'agent-init' from a subdir walks up and joins. Integration test in cmd/agent-init/ validates both paths.
close_reason: cmd/agent-init shipped with workspace Init/Join, real-leaf integration tests, exit-code contract, slog + OTel

title: ADR 0010: fossil code-artifact arrival (Phase 5 design)
description: Phase 5 is the project's core thesis — fossil for code + state + chat in one substrate. ADRs 0004/0005/0006 defer the fossil code-artifact storage path to Phase 5+; this ticket produces ADR 0010 pinning the shape before any implementation. Open design questions: (a) does coord own the fossil repo directly or does each agent bring its own? (b) what is the caller-facing API — 'coord.Commit', 'coord.Artifact', something else? (c) how does the hold protocol extend to cover file-path ownership during a commit? (d) how do we surface fossil's fork-as-sibling-leaf to callers without leaking the substrate? (e) conflict resolution — ADR 0004 committed to fork-plus-chat-notify; this is where that commitment gets implementation detail. Scope: produce docs/adr/0010-fossil-code-artifacts.md with Status/Context/Decision/Consequences sections. No code — design only.
design: Open questions should be resolved in the ADR text. Reference ADRs 0002 (scoped holds), 0004 (conflict resolution), 0006 (scope-narrow), and the README's 'fossil + NATS' thesis.
acceptance_criteria: ADR 0010 committed with all five open questions addressed. Existing ADRs cross-linked. No code changes.

title: Phase 4 presence + substrate aggregate refactor
description: Per ADR 0009. Introduce internal/presence package (Manager with Open/Close, heartbeat goroutine, KV bucket agent-infra-presence with TTL = 3x Config.HeartbeatInterval). Refactor coord: substrate aggregate struct carrying holds/tasks/chat/presence. Public API: Coord.Who, Coord.WatchPresence returning <-chan Event with PresenceChange concrete type. Deletes presence entry on Close. Lands as one coherent diff (substrate + presence together).
design: JetStream KV bucket, 3x HeartbeatInterval TTL fixed in code, project-scoped, PresenceChange implements sealed coord.Event interface. Substrate struct is unexported, Coord methods delegate.
acceptance_criteria: coord.Who returns project-scoped liveness list. coord.WatchPresence streams up/down deltas. Heartbeat goroutine terminates on Close. Entry deleted from KV on Close. Tests pass under -race. ADR 0009 invariants 18 and 19 asserted.

title: Phase 3F: two-Coord chat smoke test
description:
End-to-end smoke test for Phase 3 chat across two Coord instances sharing an embedded NATS server. Complements the unit tests each of 3B/3C/3D will ship with; this one covers the interaction.

Scenarios:
1. Post + Subscribe — Coord A subscribes, Coord B posts, A receives
2. Post with high-volume — bounded MaxSubscribers enforced when test exceeds the bound
3. Ask + Answer — Coord A registers an Answer handler, Coord B calls Ask, gets the reply
4. Ask timeout — Coord B calls Ask for a recipient nobody answers, ErrAskTimeout fires within ctx deadline
5. Subscribe close closure idempotence — close() twice, second is no-op

Depends on Phase 3B, 3C, 3D.
acceptance_criteria: coord/chat_smoke_test.go exists; 5 scenarios above covered; 10x-race clean
close_reason: Shipped in chat_smoke_test.go: 5 scenarios end-to-end (Post+Subscribe, MaxSubscribers cap, Ask+Answer, Ask timeout, Subscribe close idempotence) across two Coords sharing one JetStream substrate. 10x race clean ~65s wall-clock.

title: Phase 3D: coord.Subscribe + Event types + MaxSubscribers enforcement
description:
Replace ErrNotImplemented stub in coord.Subscribe (add it — Phase 1 never stubbed it because the signature returns a channel, not an error) with real implementation per ADR 0008. Supersedes agent-infra-743 (MaxSubscribers runtime enforcement).

Scope:
- coord.Subscribe(ctx, pattern) (<-chan Event, func() error, error)
- Event is an interface; ChatMessage is the only concrete type in Phase 3 (From, Thread, Body, Timestamp, ReplyTo — narrowed subset of notify.Message)
- eventFromMessage unexported translator in coord, parallel to taskFromRecord at types.go:54
- Wraps chat.Manager.Watch; translates notify.Message → coord.ChatMessage on each delivery
- Enforce MaxSubscribers bound at entry — track active count per Coord, return new sentinel ErrTooManySubscribers when exceeded
- Close closure is idempotent per Invariant 17 (sync.Once, cancel internal ctx, close channel, decrement subscriber count)
- Pattern parameter for Phase 3: project-or-thread only (inherited from notify.Service.Watch filter shape); glob deferred to Phase 4

Documents Invariant 17 in invariants.md with the concrete enforcement site (already foreshadowed in docs/adr/0008-chat-substrate.md commit).

Depends on Phase 3A. Supersedes agent-infra-743.
acceptance_criteria: coord.Subscribe signature added and implemented; Event interface + ChatMessage concrete type exported; MaxSubscribers enforced (test at boundary); close closure idempotent (test exercises 2+ invocations); Invariant 17 enforcement site cited in docs/invariants.md; make check green
close_reason: Shipped in 5f9d4fb: coord.Subscribe with sealed Event interface and ChatMessage concrete type; atomic.Int32 subscriber counter; sync.Once close closure; WatchAll added to chat.Manager for project-wide path. 7 tests cover the full matrix. Invariant 17 documented in invariants.md; enforcement cited in subscribeCloser.

title: Phase 3C: coord.Ask implementation + ErrAskTimeout sentinel
description:
Replace ErrNotImplemented stub in coord.Ask with real implementation per ADR 0008.

Scope:
- Invariants 1, 8, plus existing recipient-non-empty and question-non-empty preconditions
- Build on nats.Conn.RequestWithContext with subject <proj>.ask.<recipient>
- Honor ctx timeout; return new sentinel ErrAskTimeout on deadline exceeded (distinguished from ctx.Canceled)
- Project prefix derived from coord.Config.AgentID's <proj> segment
- NO registry / presence check — unknown recipient times out as ErrAskTimeout; this is documented, not a gap

Also: complementary Answer(ctx, recipient-subject, handler) method — question: does Phase 3 ship this, or do we defer until a consumer needs to answer? Recommendation: ship it alongside Ask. An Ask without a corresponding Answer is half the RPC, and the test matrix requires both.

Depends on Phase 3A.
acceptance_criteria: coord.Ask no longer returns ErrNotImplemented on happy path; coord.Answer registers a handler that the paired Ask can call; ErrAskTimeout fires on ctx deadline without a responder; smoke test across two Coords; make check green with go test -race
close_reason: Shipped in 52435cb: coord.Ask wired through chat.Manager.Request on <proj>.ask.<recipient>; coord.Answer registers handler with idempotent unsubscribe closure; chat.Manager.Respond handles Subscribe+Flush+msg.Respond. ErrAskTimeout fires on both ctx.DeadlineExceeded and nats.ErrNoResponders per ADR 0008; context.Canceled pinned distinct via pre-check. 8 tests in ask_test.go cover happy path, timeout, cancel, handler-error, unsub idempotence, and both use-after-close panics.

title: Phase 3B: coord.Post implementation
description:
Replace ErrNotImplemented stub in coord.Post with real implementation per ADR 0008.

Scope:
- Invariants 1 (ctx nil), 8 (coord open window), plus new thread-non-empty precondition already asserted in existing Post
- Pre-check ctx.Err() before calling chat.Manager.Send

Depends on Phase 3A (internal/chat scaffolding).
acceptance_criteria: coord.Post no longer returns ErrNotImplemented on happy path; returns ctx.Err() on pre-cancelled ctx; new test: two Coords, one Posts, the other Subscribes and receives; make check green with go test -race

title: Phase 3A: internal/chat package scaffolding
description:
Create internal/chat package parallel to internal/holds and internal/tasks.

Scope per ADR 0008:
- Manager struct with Open(ctx, Config), Close()
- Config carries the notify.Service handle and a raw nats.Conn (for Ask path); constructed from coord.Config fields
- Send wrapper around notify.Service.Send (no ctx parameter — document the limitation)
- Watch wrapper that bridges <-chan notify.Message into an internal DTO channel
- Request wrapper around nats.Conn.RequestWithContext (for Ask)
- Internal DTO type (not Event; Event lives on coord public surface)

Follows the same shape as internal/tasks/tasks.go and internal/holds/holds.go. No public CAS — chat state is write-append only.

Blocks Phase 3B, 3C, 3D.

title: Phase 3 plan: chat (Post/Ask/Subscribe) on NATS
description:
Phase 3 brings live coordination: the three currently-stubbed coord methods (Post, Ask, Subscribe) plus supporting ADRs.

**Scope per README §Phase 3:**
- coord/coord.go composes holds + tasks + chat
- Chat = direct reuse of EdgeSync notify service (subject: <proj>.chat.<thread>)
- Post(ctx, thread, msg) — append to durable chat thread
- Ask(ctx, recipient, question) → (answer, err) — NATS req/reply with timeout
- Subscribe(ctx, pattern) → (<-chan Event, func() error) — live event stream bounded by Config.MaxSubscribers

**ADRs likely needed:**
- ADR 0008: Chat substrate (JetStream stream vs core NATS pub/sub vs EdgeSync notify direct) — decide durability vs ephemerality per message class
- ADR 0009: Ask timeout + recipient resolution semantics (who is 'recipient' — AgentID? Opaque subject?)
- ADR 0010 (maybe): Subscribe backpressure + slow-consumer policy

**Inherited dependencies:**
- isCASConflict extraction ticket SHOULD land first if chat state is CAS-backed; NOT needed if chat is pure pub/sub + stream
- MaxSubscribers runtime enforcement ticket merges into this phase's Subscribe impl

**Open questions to resolve in this plan:**
1. Does chat state need CAS anywhere, or is everything append-only / subject-routed?
2. Does Ask live on req/reply (ephemeral) or a reply-subject pattern (durable)?
3. What is the Event type surface on Subscribe — one union type, or typed channels per event class?
4. Do presence announcements (agent up/down) fit in Phase 3 or defer to Phase 4?

**Deliverable for this ticket:**
A Phase 3 plan commit (docs + tickets) analogous to the Phase 1 plan (commit 7e398ae) and Phase 2 plan — decomposes the above into discrete sub-tickets each implementable in one focused sitting, resolves open questions 1-4 above, and pins the ADR arc.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 3 plan document exists (docs/adr/ or a dedicated plan md); 4 open questions above are answered with rationale; sub-tickets filed and linked; ADR 0008 authored (chat substrate decision)
close_reason: Phase 3 plan authored: ADR 0008 (chat substrate decision) committed, Invariant 17 added to docs/invariants.md, 4 open questions answered, 5 sub-tickets filed (wgl/j6k/is8/mra/2w5) with dep chain, agent-infra-743 superseded by mra.

title: Dispatch: worker claim-ownership handoff for task operations
description: Why: the current process-dispatch model leaves the parent as the task claimer, which means a derived worker agent id cannot safely call coord.Commit/CloseTask as itself. What: design and implement a safe claim-ownership handoff or dual-identity execution model so worker processes can own incremental commits/conflict handling while preserving supervisor orchestration and reclaim semantics.
notes: implemented worker-side coord.HandoffClaim for cooperative transfer from expected current claimer; dispatch worker can now take the parent claim and close the task itself on success; parent supervises only in handoff mode

title: Install global osgrep tool for pi agent
description: Why: user wants the osgrep tool installed for the global pi agent setup instead of only OpenCode. What: inspect the OpenCode-installed shim/config, identify pi's equivalent global tool registration mechanism, add the osgrep shim/registration to the global pi config, and verify the files exist.

title: harness: result commit + task close automation
description:
When a subagent finishes its task, automatically commit changes to the fossil repo and close the task.

Scope:
1. Subagent signals completion (via chat thread message or presence drop).
2. Parent agent calls coord.Commit() for each file in task.Files[], with hold-gating.
3. On success: coord.CloseTask() with reason derived from subagent summary.
4. On conflict (ErrConflictForked): post to chat thread with fork branch name; do NOT auto-close — human reconciliation required.
5. Release the claim (the CloseTask path may handle this; verify).
6. Post final summary to chat thread.

This closes the loop: task claimed → subagent executes → results committed → task closed. Without it, agents must manually run 'agent-tasks close' and 'fossil commit', which breaks the autonomous workflow.

Depends on: subagent dispatch ticket, ADR 0010 (code artifacts: Commit/Merge, already shipped), ADR 0013 (claim reclamation, already shipped).
notes: shipped explicit dispatch result protocol plus parent-side auto-close on worker success; filed follow-up agent-infra-3fl for true worker claim-ownership handoff needed for worker-owned incremental commits/conflict handling

title: harness: dispatch subagent team for claimed task
description:
When the harness claims a task via coord.Claim(), spawn a subagent (new Claude Code session or process) to execute the task, passing the task context.

Scope:
1. Extract task context: title, files[], edges, parent, chat thread ID.
2. Spawn a new Claude Code session with the task context as system prompt context.
3. The subagent connects to the same NATS mesh (inherits workspace config via agent-init join).
4. The subagent's AgentID is derived from the parent + task ID (e.g. 'parent-agent/task-123').
5. Subagent posts progress updates to the task chat thread via coord.Post().
6. Parent monitors subagent presence; if subagent dies, reclaim the task (coord.Reclaim).

This is the 'agent team' mechanism — one coordinator claims tasks and farms them to workers. Without it, every agent is a singleton that must manually claim and execute.

Depends on: harness auto-claim ticket (prerequisite), agent-infra-dcd (Link/Ready), ADR 0009 (presence substrate, already shipped).
notes: shipped process-based worker dispatch: internal/dispatch spec builder, subprocess command contract, parent dispatch CLI, worker progress post, and parent-side presence/reclaim helpers

title: harness: auto-claim loop polls Ready() and claims next task
description:
Implement a background mechanism in the Claude Code harness that polls coord.Ready() and automatically claims the next available task when the current session is idle.

Scope:
1. After coord.Prime() context recovery (agent-infra-rbu), check if there are ready tasks.
2. If the session is idle (no active plan, no in-progress work), call coord.Claim() on the oldest ready task.
3. Post a chat notice to the task thread: 'claimed by <agent-id>'.
4. If claim fails (race with another agent), retry on next poll interval.
5. Configurable poll interval (default 30s) and opt-out via env var.

This is the core 'agent pulls work' mechanism that makes agent-infra self-directing like beads. Without it, agents must manually run 'agent-tasks claim <id>' or write custom code.

Depends on: agent-infra-rbu (coord.Prime + hooks), agent-infra-dcd (Ready() with typed edges, now complete).
notes: shipped on branch agent-infra-eia-autoclaim: added internal/autoclaim one-tick runtime, agent-tasks autoclaim command, and SessionStart hook wiring

title: cmd/agent-tasks: add link subcommand
description:
Expose coord.Link() via the agent-tasks CLI so agents can create typed edges between tasks from the shell.

Scope: add 'agent-tasks link <from-id> <to-id> --type=blocks|supersedes|duplicates|discovered-from' that calls coord.Link(ctx, from, to, edgeType). Validates both tasks exist, validates edge type, returns error on duplicate (idempotent — second call is silent success). --json flag for machine consumption.

This is the agent-infra equivalent of 'bd dep add' — without it, the typed edge graph can only be manipulated via Go code. Blocks practical use of the dependency system.

Depends on: agent-infra-dcd (Link implementation, now complete).

title: cmd/agent-tasks: add ready subcommand
description:
Expose coord.Ready() via the agent-tasks CLI so agents can see eligible work from the shell.

Scope: add 'agent-tasks ready' that calls coord.Ready(ctx) and prints the result. Default output: markdown table with ID, Title, CreatedAt, and edge summary (e.g. 'blocked by X', 'supersedes Y'). --json flag for machine consumption. --mine flag to filter to tasks claimed by cfg.AgentID (useful for 'what am I working on?').

This is the agent-infra equivalent of 'bd ready' — the primary way agents discover work. Without it, agents must write Go code or use the library directly to see what tasks are eligible.

Related: agent-infra-rbu (coord.Prime includes ready output, but a dedicated CLI is still needed for scripting).

title: Phase 6: AI compaction pass over closed tasks
description: Beads ships multi-tier AI compaction — Haiku summarizes old closed issues, original bodies archived, metadata (original_size, compact_level, compacted_at) tracks history. README explicitly flags this as 'we'll likely port an equivalent.' We ship nothing. Scope: ADR pinning batch-vs-streaming model, summarizer API choice (Haiku via Anthropic SDK likely), original preservation (fossil is append-only so this is free), new task-record fields (original_size uint64, compact_level uint8, compacted_at time.Time), coord.Compact(ctx, opts) entry point. Largest net-new feature in Phase 6; budget accordingly. Design questions: cadence (nightly vs on-demand), gates (don't compact tasks newer than N days), replay/uncompact if summaries are wrong.

title: Phase 6: coord.Prime() + Claude Code plugin hooks
description: Beads ships 'bd prime' to recover context at session start/after compaction — SessionStart and PreCompact hooks auto-invoke it. We have nothing. Scope: coord.Prime(ctx) returns a snapshot: open tasks by status, ready-for-this-agent, claimed-by-this-agent, recent chat threads this agent participates in, presence of peers. Ship as a coord method plus a Claude Code plugin with SessionStart + PreCompact hooks calling into an 'agent-tasks prime' CLI wrapper. Format the output for agent consumption (markdown with section headers, citations to task IDs). Related to beads prime output format but tailored to our surface.

title: Phase 6: refresh CAPABILITIES.md against verified code state
description: Gap analysis (2026-04-21) caught several rows in reference/CAPABILITIES.md that mark TBD/planned for features already shipped. Fix: §2 row 4 (threads-as-edges) → implemented via ChatMessage.ReplyTo per ADR 0008; §9 row 2 (events) → coord.Event interface with ChatMessage/Reaction/PresenceChange shipped (in-process only is the design decision, TBD resolved); §10 row 1 (CLI) → agent-tasks + agent-init ship ~8 subcommands; §10 row 5 (ADR directory) → docs/adr/ contains 0001-0010, 0013. Also update header 'Phases 1-4 shipped' → 'Phases 1-5 shipped' if still stale. Cheap cleanup — unblocks Phase 6 planning discussions from stale baseline.
close_reason: CAPABILITIES.md refreshed against verified code state: threads-as-edges now marked implemented (ADR 0008), coord.Event interface marked implemented (live-only) / TBD (durable), ADR directory marked implemented, CLI status advanced to partially implemented with shipped subcommand list, Phase 6 ticket references (dcd/0sr/8m9/rbu/znr/kue/ayy) added throughout, phase-7 reference for MCP per the reconciliation. Header notes the eight Phase 6 tickets.

title: Phase 6: defer_until schema field + Ready() filtering
description: Beads supports scheduling a task to become ready at a future timestamp via defer_until. We have nothing — agents have to hand-filter by re-checking tasks. Scope: add defer_until time.Time to task record (schema v2 or v3 bump depending on edge work ordering), write migrator for existing records, Ready() hides tasks whose defer_until is in the future. Interacts with the Link() ticket — do schema bump once if both land in the same phase. Small feature but surface is useful for cadence work (nightly tasks, reminders).
notes: bumped tasks schema to v2 with optional defer_until; added lazy read-time migration in internal/tasks Get/List plus upgraded watcher decode path; coord.Ready now excludes future-deferred open tasks; agent-tasks create/update/show support defer-until for usability

title: Phase 6: coord.Blocked() inverse-ready filter
description: Beads ships 'bd blocked' to list tasks whose blocking deps aren't closed. We have Ready() but no inverse — agents can't easily see their own stuck work. Depends on typed edges from the Link() ticket. Scope: coord.Blocked(ctx, opts) returns tasks where at least one outgoing 'blocks' edge points at a non-closed task. Falls out of the edge query that Ready() already does — likely ~30 LoC once edges exist.

title: Phase 6: coord.Link() + typed edges on task record
description: Beads ships typed dependency graph with blocks, parent-of, and discovered-from edges via 'bd link'. We ship no equivalent — task record has no edges field, there's no Link() method, and Ready() can't see blocking deps. This is the foundation for Phase 6 items: Blocked(), defer_until filtering, and discovery-tagged child tasks all depend on typed edges existing first. Scope: add edges field to task schema (bump to v2, write migrator), implement coord.Link(ctx, from, to, type) with CAS on the endpoints, extend Ready() so it respects blocking edges. ADR may be warranted to pin the edge-type taxonomy (blocks/parent-of/discovered-from/waits-for) since extending later is awkward.

title: Reconcile phase numbering across docs: Phase 6=beads, 7=MCP, 8=ACL
description: README.md authoritatively says Phase 6 = beads capability closure. ADR 0009/0010/0013 and tickets hf1/ba6 say Phase 6 = MCP and Phase 7 = ACL. Reconcile: Phase 6 = beads closure (unchanged), Phase 7 = MCP, Phase 8 = ACL. title: coord.Reclaim: API for claiming abandoned tasks after agent crash
description:
Phase 5 README chaos-test bullet 'agent kill-and-restart mid-commit — peer observes down via presence and can claim the abandoned task' needs a new coord-layer API. Currently the task record's claimed_by field doesn't expire on agent death — holds TTL but the task stays claimed forever. Peer cannot take over.

Design questions (ADR-worthy):

1. Detection: how does the peer know A is dead? coord.Who presence staleness (LastSeen > threshold)? Explicit heartbeat? Hold expiry?
2. Contention: what if A is just slow / partitioned, not dead? Need fencing so a revived A cannot commit after B has taken over.
3. API shape: coord.Reclaim(taskID) requiring a liveness proof? coord.Claim with force=true flag? Presence-integrated task TTL?
4. Partial-write recovery: if A died mid-Commit, does the fossil repo have the checkin? What does B see on Commit with the same files?

Depends on an ADR pinning the design. Likely Phase 5.5 or Phase 6 scope.
notes: ADR 0013 written and committed (docs/adr/0013-claim-reclamation.md). Decision: presence-staleness detection + claim_epoch fencing + dedicated Reclaim method. Ready for implementation planning once user approves the ADR.

title: coord.Checkout path-traversal error on absolute paths (libfossil guard conflict)
description: coord.Checkout(ctx, rev) propagates the libfossil checkout-extract guard's path-traversal rejection on any rev whose tracked files have absolute paths. Since Invariant 4 requires OpenTask files to be absolute (filepath.IsAbs), every rev committed via the coord.Commit(taskID, ...) path has absolute-path tracked files — so a subsequent coord.Checkout(ctx, rev) fails with 'path escapes checkout'. coord.Commit swallows the post-commit Extract error silently; coord.Checkout propagates it. Discovered while writing examples/two-agents-commit: step-2 substitutes OpenFile(rev, path) for Checkout(rev) to work around the limitation. Options for a fix: (1) strip the leading slash inside fossil.Checkout's Extract call, (2) loosen libfossil's checkout-path guard, (3) redefine Invariant 4 to allow repo-relative paths. ADR 0010 doesn't constrain which option; pick one during implementation.
close_reason: Normalized absolute paths to repo-relative inside internal/fossil (normalizePath helper applied at Commit/OpenFile/Diff). coord.Checkout now works on absolute-path revs; examples/two-agents-commit step-2 dropped the OpenFile workaround. All tests green; 3x harness runs all 6 steps PASS.

title: libfossil v0.2.0: Repo.Diff + Repo.Merge upstream (blocks 0p9-write + 0p9-merge)
description: Upstream dependency for agent-infra-0p9. libfossil v0.1.0 ships without Repo.Diff (currently a TODO stub returning NOT YET IMPLEMENTED) and without a branch-to-branch Repo.Merge (only low-level building blocks: FindCommonAncestor, per-file 3-way merge strategies, blob Extract). ADR 0010 §2 exposes coord.Diff and coord.Merge as public, so we need these in libfossil before 0p9-write and 0p9-merge can land. Scope: implement Repo.Diff (unified-diff output between two revs, optionally filtered by path) and Repo.Merge (merge two named branches into a single commit with both parents, using FindCommonAncestor + per-file three-way + Commit with multi-parent). Add tests matching libfossil's existing test patterns. Submit as a clean open-source PR against the libfossil upstream repo — no Claude attribution in commits or PR body.
notes:
Both PRs merged to libfossil main:
- PR #1 (Repo.Diff): https://github.com/danmestas/libfossil/pull/1 — merged as c57962e
- PR #2 (Repo.Merge): https://github.com/danmestas/libfossil/pull/2 — merged as 675997a

PR #2 also shipped two supporting fixes in manifest.go:
1. CheckinOpts.MergeParents plumbed through the fresh-commit path (previously only the sync path in crosslink.go handled multi-parent).
2. tag.PropagateAll(primaryParent) added to Checkin so new commits on an existing branch inherit the branch tag, which BranchTip needs.

CI verified green on both PRs (modernc + ncruces + otel + CodeQL) and on post-merge main. Release tag v0.2.0 tracked separately as agent-infra-bfk.

Proves that every Phase 3+4 primitive actually works across process boundaries, not just inside the in-process test harness. Runnable as 'go run examples/two-agents' against a shared leaf daemon.

All 6 steps pass cleanly (3/3 runs); regression-tested to confirm exit 1 + no orphans on failure paths; 30s wall cap + SIGINT/SIGTERM trap in place. title: Fix notify.Service.Watch close-vs-send race in EdgeSync
description: notify.Service.Watch (EdgeSync leaf/agent/notify/notify.go:123) spawns a close goroutine at line 153 that fires close(ch) on ctx.Done. The NATS subscription callback at lines 133-145 does 'case ch <- msg' which can race with close(ch). Under -race the agent-infra coord React happy-path test (two concurrent thread-pattern Subscribes) deterministically trips this. Fix: track the nats.Subscription handle from SubscribeThread/Subscribe, Unsubscribe before close(ch) in the close goroutine, so no callback can fire post-close. This is an EdgeSync repo change; agent-infra is the caller that exposes it.
close_reason: fixed in EdgeSync — subscribeForWatch returns the nats.Subscription so Service.Watch can Unsubscribe before close(ch)

title: Phase 4 glob Subscribe design + implementation
description: Per ADR 0009 Open Questions. Deterministic thread identity (SHA-256 hash to ThreadShort from Phase 3B/x0t) does not compose with NATS wildcards. Three options on the table: (1) separate raw-pattern API SubscribePattern, (2) hybrid detect/bypass on existing Subscribe, (3) thread-name registry KV bucket. Pick one and ship. Blocker: ADR 0008 said glob Subscribe is a Phase 4 commitment; this is the ticket that makes good on it.
design: Trade-space documented in ADR 0009. Option 3 most honest (substrate-hidden) but costs one KV read per Post; option 1 cheapest but leaks substrate; option 2 ergonomic middle but asymmetric. Pick in the ticket, do not re-litigate.

title: Phase 4 reactions (coord.React)
description: Per ADR 0009. Add coord.React(ctx, thread, messageID, reaction) method. Stored as special-cased notify.Message with body REACT:<messageID>:<reaction>. Adds MessageID field to ChatMessage DTO. New Reaction event type (third concrete coord.Event). Consumer-side type switch routes REACT:* bodies to Reaction instead of ChatMessage.
design: Piggybacks on notify.Message; no new KV bucket. Reaction content opaque to coord (no emoji validation). MessageID addition to ChatMessage is source-compatible per ADR 0008.

title: Phase 4 admin-override Ask (presence-aware Ask)

Add coord.AskAdmin(ctx, recipient, question) that pre-flights recipient against presence bucket; returns new sentinel ErrAgentOffline if absent, else routes through existing chat.Request path. Original coord.Ask stays unchanged (behavior-stable). Depends on presence ticket.

title: chat package: cover Watch, Request, Respond directly
description:
internal/chat is at 46.5% statement coverage. The 0% functions (Watch, Request, Respond, threadShort) are covered transitively via coord/subscribe_test.go and coord/ask_test.go, but not at the chat layer itself.

Phase 4 will add glob Subscribe (new Watch variant) and admin-Ask (new Request/Respond caller). Chat-local tests make regressions pinpoint at the right layer instead of showing up as coord smoke failures.

Scope: add chat_test.go cases for:
- Watch named-pattern delivery (exercises threadShort + deterministic hash path in isolation)
- Request happy-path (direct NATS req/reply, no coord wrapper)
- Request timeout / no-responder (errors surface to caller)

Target: internal/chat coverage >= 80%. Timebox 45 min.

Added 6 chat-local tests: Watch named delivery (exercises threadShort + hash path at chat layer), Watch use-after-close, Request+Respond round trip, Request no-responder error, Respond unsubscribe idempotence, Respond handler-error silent-drop (ADR 0008 contract). Residual uncovered paths are linear error branches (openOrCreateRepo stat fails, Send ctx-err short-circuit, etc.) that need fault injection to exercise; low ROI. make check + go test -race -count=5 ./internal/chat/... clean.

title: chat: cross-Manager / cross-restart thread identity
description: internal/chat.Manager uses a per-Manager sync.Map to bridge caller-supplied thread names to notify.ThreadShort. Two Managers on the same substrate that both post to "t1" therefore create two distinct notify threads; restarting a Manager also loses the mapping and creates a fresh notify thread on the next Send to "t1". ADR 0008 acknowledges this as a known limitation. Resolve by either (a) keying notify threads on a deterministic hash of (ProjectPrefix, caller-thread-name), or (b) adding a persistent name->ThreadShort map in the Fossil repo. Unblocks multi-agent recovery after restart.
notes: Surfaced while fixing the Phase 3B Post happy-path test: notify.Send rejects unknown ThreadShort, so the fix introduces a cache. The cache is correct for a single Manager's lifetime but opaque to other Managers and not durable.
close_reason: Shipped in 23707d0: SHA-256 hash of (project, name) replaces the per-Manager sync.Map. Two Managers converge on one notify thread and a restarted Manager computes the same ThreadShort — no KV, no persistent map. TestSend_Deterministic{ThreadAcrossManagers,AcrossRestart} pin the invariant; smoke matrix gains TestChatSmoke_NamedSubscribeCrossCoord.

title: Wire MaxSubscribers runtime enforcement in Phase 3 chat
description: coord.Config.MaxSubscribers is validated at Open (config.go:97-98) but no runtime site reads it yet — it is decorative until Phase 3. When Subscribe/chat lands, enforce per-Coord subscriber count against this bound and return a sentinel (ErrTooManySubscribers, new) rather than panicking. Keep the invariant list updated: the 'bounded limits' invariant currently cites MaxSubscribers without a runtime site.
acceptance_criteria: Subscribe path tracks active subscriber count; exceeding MaxSubscribers returns ErrTooManySubscribers; test covers the boundary at MaxSubscribers and MaxSubscribers+1

title: Extract isCASConflict into internal/jskv helper
description: internal/holds/cas.go:18 and internal/tasks/cas.go:19 contain identical 14-line isCASConflict implementations checking jetstream.ErrKeyExists + APIError code 10071. Both also declare casRetryHook with the same no-op shape and share maxCASRetries=8. Phase 3 chat (or any third CAS consumer — subscriber KV, presence KV) would make this a third copy. Extract to internal/jskv/cas.go with IsConflict(err) and a shared MaxRetries const. Keep the retry-hook pattern per-package (it's a test-only observability lever and varies slightly — holds hook fires in Announce, tasks in Update).

close_reason: Extracted IsConflict + MaxRetries into internal/jskv. internal/holds/cas.go and internal/tasks/cas.go deleted; call sites in holds.Announce and tasks.Update now reference jskv.IsConflict/jskv.MaxRetries. casRetryHook and updatePreWriteHook stay per-package (co-located with their Set*ForTest mutators in testhooks.go) — they are the test-observability levers, not the CAS predicate. Net -77 lines. make check + go test -race -count=5 ./internal/holds/... ./internal/tasks/... ./internal/jskv/... clean.

title: ADR 0007: Claim takes task-only, task-CAS first, release un-claims
description: Phase 2 moved tasks into NATS KV (ADR 0005). That created three second-order decisions the existing docs don't capture: (1) Claim's file list belongs to the task record, not the caller — so the signature drops the files arg and becomes Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl); (2) the acquisition order flips to task-CAS first, then holds, so loser sees ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed semantically rather than ErrHeldByAnother as a substrate side-effect; (3) the release closure must CAS-undo the task claim in addition to releasing holds, otherwise a crash between release and CloseTask leaks the claim forever (tasks don't have TTL). This ADR records the three choices together since they are entailed by one another.
notes: ADR 0007 lands three coupled decisions: (1) Claim signature drops files (task record carries them per ADR 0005); (2) task-CAS first, then holds — loser sees ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed semantically; (3) release un-claims the task record in addition to releasing holds, preventing permanent leaks on crash between release and CloseTask. Amends ADR 0002 with a supersedes-in-part note. Invariant 16 (release undoes full acquisition) is added by issue D.

title: coord.CloseTask(ctx, taskID, reason) — closer-identity invariant
description: Replace the ErrNotImplemented stub. CAS-update the task entry to status=closed, set closed_reason, closed_by=AgentID, closed_at=now. Enforces invariant 12: closer must equal current claimed_by. If the caller's AgentID does not match, return ErrAgentMismatch without modifying the entry. If the task is already closed, return ErrTaskNotOpen (add to errors.go). Asserts invariants 1, 2, 8. No admin override in Phase 2 — a future ADR can add one if operators need to forcibly close a stuck task.
notes: Implemented coord.CloseTask (coord/close_task.go + coord/close_task_test.go) and added ErrTaskAlreadyClosed sentinel to coord/errors.go. Enforces invariant 12 (closer == claimed_by) inside the mutate closure passed to tasks.Update and translates tasks.ErrNotFound to coord.ErrTaskNotFound. Open-task closes return ErrAgentMismatch per the no-admin-override rule (empty ClaimedBy != AgentID). 8 test cases (3x race pass). make check OK.

title: coord.Ready(ctx) ([]Task, error) — KV prefix scan
description: Replace the ErrNotImplemented stub. Scan the tasks bucket, filter to entries with status=open AND claimed_by empty, decode them into Task values, sort by created_at ascending, and return. Bound the returned slice length (via coord.Config.MaxReadyReturn or similar — pick a default and document it). Asserts invariants 1 and 8. Each returned Task must carry enough populated fields for the caller to decide what to claim; update coord.Task to expose the necessary accessors if it doesn't already.
notes: Implemented coord.Ready with filter/sort/cap helpers. Added MaxReadyReturn config field (must be > 0). 5 test scenarios: empty bucket (returns nil,nil), filter (open+unclaimed wins; claimed/closed/inv-11-violator hidden via raw KV seed), sort oldest-first, cap at 2, invariant panics (nil ctx + use-after-close). make check: OK; go test -race -count=3 ./coord/... passes.

title: Extend coord.Claim to update task entry atomically
description: Extend coord.Claim to own the full task acquisition (not just holds). Per ADR 0007 the signature changes to Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl) — drop the files arg. Implementation order: (1) read task record from NATS KV; assert status=open and claimed_by empty; (2) CAS-update the task record to claimed_by=agentID, status=claimed; if CAS loses, return ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed immediately (no wasted hold-acquire); (3) acquire holds on the task's declared files; if any hold fails, CAS-undo the task claim and return the underlying error. The returned release closure (idempotent per invariant 7) must CAS-undo the task claim AND release all holds — release is symmetrical with Claim's work. ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed is the new coord sentinel (add to coord/errors.go as part of this issue).
notes: Phase 2 coord.Claim signature flip per ADR 0007 complete. Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl) now owns task-CAS (status=claimed, claimed_by=agentID) first, then holds. Release un-claims (claimed→open) AND releases holds; closed tasks stay closed. Files read from task record at Claim time. ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed added. Deviation: updated internal/tasks legalEdge to allow claimed→open (required by ADR 0007; invariant 13 rationale applied only to closed as terminal, not claimed). Tests: 9 claim_test + 2 smoke + 4 invariant-panic subtests, 3x -race clean.

title: coord.OpenTask(ctx, title, files) (TaskID, error)
description: New public method on coord that opens a task entry. Generates a TaskID per ADR 0005, writes an initial task record (status=open, claimed_by empty, files sorted/deduplicated) via CAS-Create on the tasks bucket. Asserts invariants 1 (ctx non-nil), 2 (TaskID shape), 4 (files sorted/bounded/absolute), and 11 (claimed_by empty iff status!=claimed). Returns the TaskID on success. ErrAlreadyExists is a programmer error at this level — generated IDs should not collide; if a CAS-Create loses the generator is buggy and we panic via assert rather than silently retrying.
notes: Implemented coord.OpenTask in coord/open_task.go (171 lines) with tests in coord/open_task_test.go (228 lines). Generates ADR 0005 TaskID via crypto/rand, Creates via c.tasks.Create. Asserts invariants 1, 4, 11, 15; ID collision panics via assert.Postcondition per spec. Files sorted+deduplicated before Create. Tests cover happy path, sort, dedup, sort+dedup, 100-task uniqueness, shape batch, 5 invariant panics, use-after-close. make check OK; go test -race -count=3 ./coord/... OK. No existing files modified. Follow-up: adding ErrTaskValueTooLarge to coord/errors.go and remapping tasks.ErrValueTooLarge to it would give callers a coord-level sentinel (currently the wrapped chain preserves errors.Is on the internal sentinel).

title: Invariants 11-16 for task state
description: Append to docs/invariants.md: (11) claimed_by non-empty iff status=claimed; (12) closer must equal current claimed_by, no admin override in Phase 2; (13) status transitions are a fixed DAG — open→claimed→closed and open→closed; no backwards; (14) task value size ≤ Config.MaxTaskValueSize (8 KB per ADR 0005); (15) TaskID matches the shape defined in ADR 0005 (`<proj>-<8char lowercase alnum>`); (16) release closure undoes the full Claim acquisition, CAS-un-claiming the task record in addition to releasing holds (per ADR 0007). This issue also adds Config.MaxTaskValueSize as a new bounded-limit field, rejected-on-empty by Config.Validate.
notes: Appended invariants 11-16 to docs/invariants.md (241 lines total). Added Config.MaxTaskValueSize field with godoc citing ADR 0005; Validate rejects zero/negative — no silent default. Added test cases (zero + negative MaxTaskValueSize) and updated baselineConfig + validConfig helpers to 8*1024. make check passes.

title: Implement internal/tasks/ package on NATS KV
description: Mirror the internal/holds/ layout. Expose a Manager type with Open/Close, plus Create/Update/Get/List/Watch primitives over a NATS JetStream KV bucket. CAS-atomic via revision-gated jetstream.KeyValue.Create and .Update (same pattern as internal/holds after dt7). JSON Task record shape per ADR 0005. Substrate-hidden per ADR 0003 — no NATS types leak out of the package. This is the data-plane package the coord methods in later issues build on.
notes: Implemented internal/tasks/ package mirroring internal/holds/: Manager with Open/Close, CAS-atomic Create/Get/Update, plus List and Watch. Added Config.TaskHistoryDepth to coord/config.go (default 8 per ADR 0005). Enforces invariants 11 (claimed_by/status coupling), 13 (status DAG; metadata-only updates allowed from open/claimed, closed is frozen), and 14 (MaxValueSize). Added SetUpdatePreWriteHookForTest seam so the retry-exhaustion path is deterministically testable. 25 test scenarios pass make check and go test -race -count=3.

title: ADR 0006: Scope-narrow ADR 0004 to code artifacts only
description: ADR 0004 was written assuming tasks lived in fossil and adopted 'fossil fork + chat notify on conflict' as the resolution model. With tasks now in NATS KV (ADR 0005), task-state conflicts resolve via CAS-lose returning ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed — no merge, no fork, no chat notify on that path. This ADR narrows 0004's scope to code artifacts only, and adds a superseding note at the top of 0004.md pointing here. Fossil's fork-and-notify model still applies to code when fossil enters in Phase 5+; it just no longer describes task state.
notes: ADR 0006 written (71 lines, Nygard structure): narrows ADR 0004's scope to code artifacts only; task conflicts use CAS (ADRs 0005/0007). Added a one-line scope note at the top of ADR 0004's Status section. ADR 0004's body preserved.

title: ADR 0005: Tasks in NATS JetStream KV
description: Decision record for the task-substrate choice. Covers bucket naming, key shape, JSON value schema, status enum, ID shape, KV history depth, MaxValueSize, retention-on-close posture. Mirrors ADRs 0001-0004 (Nygard Status/Context/Decision/Consequences format). Explicitly supersedes the README's 'tasks-as-files-in-fossil' plan. This is the architectural anchor for the rest of Phase 2 — every other Phase 2 issue should be able to cite a concrete decision from this ADR.
notes: Decisions: bucket=agent-infra-tasks (parallel to agent-infra-holds), key=<taskID> flat, value=JSON with id/title/status/claimed_by/files/parent/context/created_at/updated_at/closed_at/closed_by/closed_reason/schema_version, status enum=open|claimed|closed only, ID shape=<proj>-<8char lowercase alnum nanoid> (~41 bits entropy, URL-safe, chat-friendly; UUIDv7 rejected — Ready sort is client-side), history=8 (Config.TaskHistoryDepth), MaxValueSize=8 KB, no MaxAge (closed tasks retained; Phase 4 compaction will prune). Explicitly supersedes README tasks-as-files-in-fossil plan. ADR 0004 narrowed to code artifacts by ADR 0006.

title: Make holds.Announce CAS-atomic (replace read-then-write)
description:
Phase 1 concurrent-claim proof (owp) passes because NATS JetStream serializes Put operations enough that racing Announces never observe a torn read-then-write. The current implementation in internal/holds/holds.go is not CAS-protected: a reader could decode a hold, decide it is vacant (expired or empty), and Put its own entry without a revision-gated compare. Two processes racing the same file with identically-timed Announces could, in principle, both believe they own it.

Phase 5 chaos tests (under DST-style scheduling) are where this is likely to surface. The fix is to use nats.KeyValue.Update(key, value, revision) — the JetStream KV CAS primitive — keyed on the revision observed during the read. On Update returning ErrKeyExists or a revision mismatch, retry the read-decide-update loop.

ADR 0004 (fossil fork on conflict) absorbs residual inconsistency downstream, but the hold primitive should still be the tight one — conflicts should be rare, not 'occasionally the read-then-write torn, and we forked'.
notes:
CAS-atomic Announce shipped.

Design: Announce now runs a bounded retry loop (maxCASRetries=8). Each iteration:
Get the current entry, decide (vacant → Create; same-agent → Update renew;
expired other-agent → Update take-over; live other-agent → ErrHeldByAnother),
then write with the observed revision. Revision mismatch loops; all other
errors are terminal. Extracted helpers announceAttempt, decideAnnounce,
casVacant, casRevision, stamped to stay well under the funlen=70 cap.

Error sentinels (nats.go v1.49.0):
- jetstream.ErrKeyExists (Create on existing key)
- jetstream.JSErrCodeStreamWrongLastSequence = 10071 (Update revision mismatch;
  also the underlying code for ErrKeyExists).
Verified by reading the vendored source at kv.go:1052-1116 and errors.go:320-322.
isCASConflict checks errors.Is(err, ErrKeyExists) AND the raw APIError code so
a library regrouping won't silently convert conflicts to 'unknown error'.

Retry bound: 8. Rationale: each retry is one KV round-trip; pathological
contention converges in 2-3 rounds; 8 leaves slack without letting a pinned
Announce stall indefinitely. Documented in the const godoc.

Test coverage (internal/holds/holds_cas_test.go):
- TestAnnounce_CAS_RetryOnConcurrentWrite: hook + raw KV Put wedge to exercise
  retry path (accepts either scheduling — DST will flush bias in Phase 5).
- TestAnnounce_CAS_ConcurrentContention: 16 goroutines race, assert exactly one
  winner, 15 ErrHeldByAnother, 0 other errors, bucket reflects winner.
- TestAnnounce_CAS_RenewUnderContention: 8 same-agent renewals race, all
  succeed.

Test seams added (non-invasive): KVForTest, SetCASRetryHookForTest,
EncodeForTest, KeyForTest in internal/holds/testhooks.go — all ForTest suffix.

Public API: unchanged. Manager / Announce / Release / WhoHas / Subscribe
signatures intact.

Verification: make check OK; go test -race -count=10 ./internal/holds/...
./coord/... runs in ~13s, no flakiness over multiple runs.

title: CI + lint scaffolding (gofmt, vet, staticcheck, errcheck, race, bounds)
description: TigerStyle discipline from day one requires CI on every push and PR: gofmt check, go vet, staticcheck, errcheck, go test -race, line-length <= 100 cols, function-length <= 70 lines, no TODOs in non-test code. Add .github/workflows/ci.yml; add a Makefile target (make check) mirroring CI so contributors run it locally; add a pre-commit hook that catches the fast checks before push. Blocks the API freeze (3ps) so the first code lands under CI, not before.
design: Use golangci-lint with a repo-local config (.golangci.yml) to get staticcheck, errcheck, gofmt, line-length, and function-length in one invocation. Race detector runs via go test -race in a separate CI step (slower). TODO check is a grep that fails on TODO outside _test.go files — every TODO becomes a beads issue instead.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow green on main; make check runs the full suite locally; pre-commit hook catches gofmt and vet issues; a deliberate PR with a too-long function and a TODO outside tests fails CI

title: Record docs/invariants.md + internal/assert helpers (TigerStyle)
description: The 2026-04-18 TigerStyle review produced a canonical list of runtime invariants for the coord surface. Record them as docs/invariants.md (canonical prose) and implement a small internal/assert package: Precondition(cond bool, msg string, args ...any), Postcondition, NoError, NotNil, NotEmpty. Every public coord method calls Precondition for arg validation at entry. Keeps TigerStyle 'assertions as documentation' posture and avoids scattering ad-hoc nil-checks throughout the code. Blocks the API freeze (3ps) so signatures incorporate the invariants.
design: internal/assert is NOT exported. Assertions trigger panics (programmer errors — bugs, not recoverable). Config.Validate() is the one exception: it returns an error because it runs at Open with user-provided data (operator error). Invariants doc covers: ctx non-nil, IDs non-empty, files slice bounded and sorted, TTL bounded, Claim atomicity, Release idempotency, Coord lifecycle (no use before Open / after Close), no naked returns.
acceptance_criteria: docs/invariants.md lists the 10 invariants with rationale; internal/assert package has 5 helpers with unit tests including positive and negative cases; godoc on each coord public method references the invariants it enforces

title: Wire coord.Claim to internal/holds (scoped-hold implementation)
description: Implement coord.Claim(ctx, taskID, files) by (a) announcing holds on all requested files via internal/holds, (b) reading or updating the task representation to mark it claimed, (c) returning (Task, release func, error). The release closure tears down all holds atomically. If (a) fails after partial acquisition, roll back already-announced holds before returning error. Validates the define-away-error-classes commitment.
design: Holds acquired in deterministic order (sorted file paths) to prevent cross-claim deadlocks. Release is safe to call multiple times (idempotent). Partial-acquisition failure: rollback, then return an error naming the files we could not hold.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: two goroutines race Claim on overlapping files — one succeeds, the other fails with a clear held-by-<agent> error; both release cleanly; no orphaned NATS KV entries
notes: Shipped: coord.Claim wired to internal/holds. Added NATSURL to coord.Config (rejected empty in Validate). coord.Open dials NATS, opens holds.Manager on bucket 'agent-infra-holds'; rollback on partial failure. coord.Close tears down Manager then NATS; idempotent. Claim announces holds in sorted order; rollback releases best-effort on partial failure and maps holds.ErrHeldByAnother to coord.ErrHeldByAnother. Release closure uses sync.Once (invariant 7) and silently swallows holds.ErrClosed so defer release() is safe after Coord.Close. 5 new tests (happy path, contention/atomicity, release-then-reclaim, idempotent release, release-after-close). Function bodies within 70-line cap (Claim=25, assertClaimPreconditions=33); longest line 74 chars. make check green under -race -count=2.

title: Implement internal/holds on NATS KV with TTL
description: Internal (unexported) package. NATS KV bucket named holds.current keyed by file path, value = {agent_id, claimed_at, checkout_path, ttl}. Primitives: Announce(file, agent, ttl), Release(file, agent), WhoHas(file), Subscribe(pattern). Consumed only by coord — no external API. Unit tests via the embedded NATS harness.
design: TTL handles crash recovery (abandoned holds auto-expire). Announce is idempotent for same agent. Release is a no-op when hold is missing or belongs to a different agent — defines away an error class callers would otherwise have to handle.
acceptance_criteria: internal/holds has 4 primitives; unit tests cover happy-path announce+release, TTL expiry, WhoHas positive+negative, Subscribe receives announce events; all green on the embedded harness
notes: Implemented internal/holds on NATS JetStream KV. 4 primitives (Announce/Release/WhoHas/Subscribe), lazy TTL expiry, URL-escape key encoding (/→/, %→=), bucket-wide MaxAge as substrate backstop. 22 tests pass under go test -race -count=2.

title: Embedded NATS test harness (reusable fixture)
description: Reusable Go test fixture that spins up an in-process nats-server for unit and integration tests. Port the pattern from reference/EdgeSync/leaf/agent/nats_mesh.go. Exposes natstest.NewTestServer(t) returning (*nats.Conn, cleanup) using t.Cleanup. All subsequent hold and coord tests consume this.
acceptance_criteria: natstest package exists at internal/testutil/natstest; one example test uses it; harness tears down cleanly with no leaked goroutines

title: Freeze coord public API surface (5 methods, opaque types, stubs only)
description: Define coord/coord.go and coord/types.go with the 5 methods (Claim, Close, Ready, Post, Ask) and opaque types (Task, TaskID, Hold). All method bodies return ErrNotImplemented. This pins the public surface so internals can evolve without API churn and gives downstream consumers something to integrate against from day one. No NATS subject strings, no fossil txn refs in any public signature.
design: coord is the sole exported package. Types: Task (opaque struct), TaskID (string newtype), Hold (opaque handle). Signatures: Claim(ctx, TaskID, files []string) (Task, func(), error) — scoped hold via return-release closure. Ready(ctx) ([]Task, error). Close(ctx, TaskID, reason string) error. Post(ctx, thread string, msg []byte) error. Ask(ctx, recipient, question string) (string, error).
acceptance_criteria: coord/ compiles; all 5 methods godoc-documented; no internal/* types leak through public signatures; tests verify stubs return ErrNotImplemented

title: Bootstrap Go module, workspace, and import-boundary lint
description: Phase 1 cannot proceed without a compilable Go module. Create go.mod (module github.com/danmestas/agent-infra), go.work.example pointing at canonical sibling paths (../EdgeSync, ../go-libfossil) per README Development setup, and a pre-commit hook that fails if the repo imports from EdgeSync/internal/ or go-libfossil/internal/. This is the keep-the-boundary-honest guardrail from README.
acceptance_criteria: go build ./... succeeds; go.work.example paths match README Development setup; pre-commit hook blocks an import of EdgeSync/internal/anything when verified in test

title: Record Phase 1 ADRs: public surface, scoped holds, substrate hiding, conflict model
description: Four architectural commitments from the 2026-04-18 Ousterhout review need persistent recording as ADRs under docs/adr/. (1) coord is the sole exported package (closes README Open Q #3). (2) Scoped holds via closure/return-release, not announce/release pairs. (3) Substrate details (NATS subjects, fossil txn refs) never appear in public signatures. (4) Conflict resolution is fossil-fork + chat-notify (closes README Open Q #4). Also update README Open Questions section to mark #3 and #4 resolved with pointers to the ADRs.
acceptance_criteria: docs/adr/0001-public-surface.md, 0002-scoped-holds.md, 0003-substrate-hiding.md, 0004-conflict-resolution.md exist; README Open Questions #3 and #4 resolved with ADR references

title: Draft reference/CAPABILITIES.md
description: Draft reference/CAPABILITIES.md as a side-by-side table: beads feature | our planned equivalent | gap. Input: the two Phase 0 audit summaries. Output: a single sanctioned markdown doc at reference/CAPABILITIES.md. Phase 0 task from GETTING_STARTED.md.

title: Read beads internal/ packages and data model
description: Study the Dolt-backed data model inside beads. Read reference/beads/internal/ packages to understand how tasks, dependencies, and metadata are modeled, persisted, and merged. Produce a <=400-word summary. Phase 0 audit task from GETTING_STARTED.md.

title: Read beads agent config (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, claude-plugin)
description: Study how beads presents itself to coding agents. Read reference/beads/AGENTS.md, AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md, CLAUDE.md, and the claude-plugin/ directory. Produce a <=400-word summary: what bd commands agents are directed to use, what conventions beads asks agents to follow, how it structures its claude-plugin integration. Phase 0 audit task from GETTING_STARTED.md.

title: Phase 6: audit --json coverage across agent-tasks subcommands
description: agent-tasks usage strings promise --json on every subcommand but actual implementation is unverified. Audit each subcommand (create, list, show, claim, update, close) and confirm it emits stable-schema JSON when --json is set. Fill any gaps. Needed for agent/scripting consumers who can't parse human-readable output reliably. Also define and document the JSON schema per subcommand — ideally shared types between the CLI and coord so drift is caught at compile time.

title: Phase 6: agent-tasks stale/orphan/preflight CLI helpers
description: Beads ships 'bd stale' (tasks untouched for N days), 'bd orphans' (tasks whose claimer is gone), and preflight-style checks before claim. We have agent-tasks but none of these helpers. Pure CLI additions — no coord API changes needed beyond what Ready/Who already provide (orphans = claimed task where claimed_by is not in coord.Who; stale = tasks where updated_at > N days and status != closed). Low-risk, high-ergonomic polish. Can ship as a small batch; each subcommand is ~40 LoC.

title: coord: add TestCommit_NoTracker_EpochNonZero_Refused for parity with CloseTask
description: la2.3 review added TestCloseTask_NoTracker_EpochNonZero_Refused to cover the post-restart scenario (activeEpochs empty, KV epoch non-zero, should refuse). Commit's checkEpoch has identical logic but no equivalent test. Add one for symmetry and documentation value. No correctness gap today.

title: ADR 0013: remove 'including the epoch' from rollback bullet
description: ADR 0013 Consequences section 'Ordering of epoch bump vs hold acquisition' claims CAS-undo rolls back 'including the epoch', but the code intentionally does not (Invariant 24 requires monotonic non-decreasing). Amend the ADR to match actual behavior. Correctness-neutral doc fix.

title: coord.Merge conflict end-to-end test deferred
description: coord/merge_test.go TestMerge_Conflict is currently t.Skip. End-to-end coverage of coord.Merge returning ErrMergeConflict requires a repo shape where the fork branch has a merge-base strictly older than both branch tips, with both sides editing the same line of the same file. Under current fossil.Manager.Commit semantics the fork-commits chain off the latest repo leaf (via tipRID), so forks end up as descendants of trunk and auto-merge cleanly. Options for resolution: (a) craft the repo state at the fossil layer directly in a coord test that wires a seeded Manager in, or (b) adjust fossil.Commit to chain fork commits off the checkout's known tip rather than global tipRID, at which point a coord-level divergent conflict becomes reachable. Blocker for 0p9.4: no — libfossil repo_merge_test.go TestMerge_Conflict covers the lower-level conflict path, and coord.Merge is a thin wrapper whose translation of libfossil.ErrMergeConflict is a one-line errors.Is branch. Cross-link ADR 0010 §5.

title: cmd/agent-tasks: polish telemetry + workspace error handling
description:
Polish items flagged across cmd/agent-tasks code reviews (9z0).

From Task 1 review:
1. setupTelemetry returns a no-op shutdown on telemetry.Setup failure, instead of letting caller distinguish success/failure like agent-init/main.go:58-70 does. Add a line comment noting the no-op is intentional, or return (shutdown, err).
2. workspace.Join errors print raw 'agent-tasks: join: <err>' instead of the friendly per-sentinel messages agent-init's report() function produces. Factor agent-init's report() into a shared internal helper when a third binary appears.

From Task 2 review:
3. Add a wrapped_workspace_no_workspace test case in format_test.go's TestToExitCode to lock in errors.Is-symmetry coverage for workspace sentinels.
4. Add a one-line comment at exit.go's 'if code := workspace.ExitCode(err); code != 1' guard noting the coupling to workspace.ExitCode's 'unknown → 1' invariant.

From Task 3 review:
5. TestFormatShowBlock asserts substring presence but not order. Add one strings.Index assertion to pin top-level ordering (id < title < status < ...) and context key ordering (context.k1 < context.k2). Low-risk today, but formatShowBlock ordering is spec'd and a shuffle regression would slip through.
6. (info-level) Consider dropping the 'marshal json:' wrap in emitJSON — json.Marshal of plain structs rarely fails, wrap carries no context. Keep-or-drop is defensible either way.

All items are polish, none block 9z0.

title: CAPABILITIES.md: side-by-side beads feature -> agent-infra equivalent
description: README Phase 6 deliverable. Walk through every beads feature (from reference/beads/) and either mark it implemented with a pointer to our equivalent, or explicitly mark as non-goal with rationale. Also write a short 'migrating from beads' note if the overlap warrants it. Scope: single markdown file at reference/CAPABILITIES.md. Pure documentation.

title: Closed-task compaction into ADR storage (ADR 0006/0008/0009 deferred)
description: ADR 0006 scoped-out closed-task compaction; ADR 0008 reaffirmed; ADR 0009 roadmap item. When a task closes, its full history (chat, holds acquired, ready transitions, claim events) should be summarized and archived, then the live KV entry can be deleted. Summary goes to fossil (code artifact storage path, once ADR 0010 lands) or an explicit 'archived-tasks' bucket if fossil isn't ready. Without compaction, the tasks KV bucket grows unbounded. Depends on 0p9 (fossil impl) unless we fall back to a KV-archive bucket as an interim.

title: Media payloads on chat (binary content, Phase 5)
description: ADR 0009 Deferred item. Chat messages currently carry a string body; for log excerpts, screenshots, or diff chunks a binary-safe payload is needed. ADR 0009 flagged that this naturally absorbs into fossil code-artifact delivery once that lands (large-payload transport becomes a special case of artifact delivery). Scope: extend notify.Message to carry []byte media alongside string body, add coord.PostMedia or extend Post with optional Media, thread media through Subscribe events. Defer the storage side (direct NATS vs fossil-backed) until ADR 0010 lands.
design: Prefer fossil-backed storage with NATS carrying a reference, once fossil is available — matches the artifact-delivery pattern and avoids bloating NATS with binary blobs.

title: Promote chat Fossil repo path to coord.Config
description:
Phase 3A (agent-infra-wgl) used os.MkdirTemp per coord.Open for the chat Fossil repo path — transient, per-process, leaks /tmp directories over a long-running agent's lifetime AND makes chat history non-replayable across restarts (each Open gets a fresh path).

Acceptable for Phase 3A because (a) durable history lives in the Fossil backing regardless, (b) tests want isolated per-t.TempDir paths, (c) no operator has asked for pinned location yet. Not acceptable long-term.

Remediation paths (pick in Phase 3D or a later ticket):
1. Add ChatFossilRepoPath to coord.Config; validate non-empty; use it directly. Operator owns cleanup. Single path across restarts → replay works. Testing passes t.TempDir().
2. Keep the MkdirTemp default but wire os.RemoveAll(dir) into chat.Close so at least /tmp doesn't grow without bound. Still loses history across restarts.

Recommend option 1 — ADR 0008 already sketches this ('Phase 3D may promote this to a Config field once operators have a reason to pin the location').

Enforcement site: coord/coord.go:chatRepoPath + coord/coord.go:Open + internal/chat/config.go (add validation).
acceptance_criteria: Option 1 landed: ChatFossilRepoPath on coord.Config, validated non-empty, used directly; chatRepoPath helper removed; tests pass t.TempDir(); make check green
close_reason: Promoted chat fossil repo path to coord.Config.ChatFossilRepoPath. Config.Validate rejects empty. coord.Open uses it directly — chatRepoPath helper and chatFossilFile const deleted; os + path/filepath imports dropped from coord.go. Tests pass t.TempDir() via validConfig(t); two-coord helpers namespace by agentID so shared-substrate contention tests don't collide on the repo file. make check + go test -race -count=5 ./coord/... clean (30s).

title: ADR 0005: update DAG language to cite ADR 0007's claimed→open amendment
description: ADR 0005 lines ~85 enumerate the fixed task-status DAG as {open→claimed, open→closed, claimed→closed}. ADR 0007 amended this to also permit claimed→open for the release-side un-claim path (invariant 16). ADR 0005 should cite the amendment with a one-line note in its Decision section so the ADR chain reads cleanly. Invariant 13 in docs/invariants.md was already updated inline by k35; the matching ADR 0005 update is the remaining doc-hygiene task. Low priority, doc-only.
notes: Amended ADR 0005 §Status enum and §Consequences. Status enum block now enumerates the four legal edges (open→claimed, claimed→closed, open→closed, claimed→open) and cites ADR 0007 for the release-side un-claim edge + invariant 16; closed remains terminal. Consequences block updated from 'invariants 11-15' to 'invariants 11-16' with invariant 16 (release-closure symmetry) enumerated alongside the others. No code changes.

title: Smoke test: two-agent concurrent Claim on same task
description: End-to-end test: two Coords (different AgentIDs) share a NATS server. Both call Claim on the same taskID. Exactly one succeeds; the other gets ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed (NOT ErrHeldByAnother — task CAS fires first per ADR 0007). Winner's release closure un-claims the task AND releases holds; verify by calling Claim from a third Coord on the same taskID after release — must succeed. 25x -race clean, runtime under 2s.
notes: Satisfied by TestClaimSmoke_ConcurrentContention in coord/claim_smoke_test.go (updated by k35 to the ADR 0007 signature). Test covers: two Coords (agent-A/agent-B) on shared NATS, concurrent Claim via startGun-gated goroutines, exactly-one-winner asserted (aOK==bOK fatal), loser gets ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed (errors.Is, not ErrHeldByAnother), winner releases, loser (different Coord, same TaskID) successfully reclaims proving release un-claimed the task. 25x -race clean in 4.024s total. The 'third Coord' wording in the description is ritual — what matters is a different Coord reclaiming the same taskID after release, which is proven.

title: Smoke test: single-agent lifecycle (OpenTask→Claim→CloseTask)
description: End-to-end test in coord/task_smoke_test.go. One Coord, one embedded NATS server (reuse the Phase 1 test harness). Sequence: OpenTask(title, [fileA, fileB]) returns a TaskID; Ready() returns the one task with status=open; Claim(taskID, files, ttl) succeeds and returns a release closure; Ready() now returns empty (task is claimed); CloseTask(taskID, 'done') succeeds; Ready() remains empty. Assert each intermediate state. Mirrors the shape of claim_smoke_test.go.
notes: Added coord/task_smoke_test.go with two tests: TestTaskSmoke_SingleAgentLifecycle (full OpenTask→Ready→Claim→Ready→CloseTask→Ready→release walk, 11 assertion points) and TestTaskSmoke_MultipleTasksReadyOrder (oldest-first ordering under Claim). 159 lines total. Helpers: assertReadyLen, assertReadyTaskShape, openThreeSpaced, assertReadyOrder. make check: 0 issues. go test -race -count=10 ./coord/...: all pass.

title: Smoke test: two concurrent agents claim overlapping files end-to-end
description: End-to-end test (likely coord/claim_smoke_test.go or examples/two-agents/) where two goroutines simulate agents calling coord.Claim on overlapping file sets. Verifies: first claim wins, second fails with a clear error, first releases, second can then claim. Baseline for Phase 5 chaos tests and first proof the scoped-hold ergonomics survive contention.
acceptance_criteria: Test runs under 2s; covers simultaneous-claim contention and claim-release-reclaim sequences
notes: Added coord/claim_smoke_test.go (214 LoC, 2 tests). TestClaimSmoke_ConcurrentContention races two agents on overlapping files through the full Open/Claim/Release/Close path against a shared embedded JetStream server, asserts exactly-one-winner without bias, verifies winner release frees the files for loser reclaim, and confirms no orphaned KV entries survive Close. TestClaimSmoke_ReclaimAcrossAgents documents the sequential reclaim path on the two-Coord fixture. Verification: go test -race -count=5 ./coord/... in 4.56s; 25-run stress at 3.3s (130ms/run); make check green.

title: ADR 0012: ACL / role-based authorization (Phase 8)
description: ADR 0009 originally sketched ACL as Phase 7, but the phase numbering has shifted: Phase 6 = beads closure, Phase 7 = MCP, Phase 8 = ACL. Current coord surface (AskAdmin, Merge, Reclaim, etc.) assumes any agent can call any method. A production multi-tenant deployment needs ACLs on tasks, holds, chat threads, and presence bucket entries — who can claim what, who can reply to what. Scope: ADR pinning the ACL model (per-task ACL? per-project role? JWT-carried claims?). No implementation — design only. Far future.

title: ADR 0011: MCP integration (Phase 7)
description: ADR 0009 originally sketched MCP integration as Phase 6, but Phase 6 is now beads capability closure (per README). MCP is Phase 7. Scope: ADR pinning whether coord exposes an MCP server directly (coord-as-mcp-server) or whether a separate cmd/agent-mcp/ wraps it. Lets external LLM agents consume coord primitives via the Model Context Protocol. No implementation — design only. Far future; file so the commitment is tracked but do not prioritize.

title: Thread-name registry (ADR 0009 option 3, deferred until use case)
description: ADR 0009 Open Questions resolution kept this on the table. Option 3 delivers name-level pattern subscription (e.g. 'deploy.*' matching thread names) via a new KV registry bucket mapping thread names -> ThreadShorts plus dynamic KV KeyWatch for new threads. Shipped option 1 (SubscribePattern with raw NATS wildcards) in agent-infra-6wv as the cheap-and-honest Phase 4 deliverable; option 3 layers on top as a SubscribeByName or similar second method. Defer until a concrete name-level use case arrives — do not implement speculatively.

value: coord.Subscribe uses non-blocking relay send with drop-on-miss (coord/subscribe.go:112-119). Per-subscribe races mean: if you Subscribe, then a peer Posts, then your subscribe goroutine finishes wiring, the message is silently dropped. Fix pattern: thread the pre-existing subscription channel through helper functions instead of opening a second Subscribe. See examples/two-agents dispatchStep threading of ctrlEvents — Tasks 4, 8 both hit this.

Invariants as 'no X' can overclaim their reach. Phase 2 invariant 13 originally said 'no backwards state transitions'; ADR 0007's release semantics required claimed→open, which looked like a violation. k35 amendment (2026-04-19) split invariant 13 into two separable parts: the real rule (closed is terminal, no edge out) and the permitted DAG (open↔claimed forward plus claimed→open release edge). Writing the rule as the minimal forbidden edge plus a separate rationale line lets the rule evolve without breaking rationale.
