Phase 1 bounded limits (TigerStyle, 2026-04-18) — all live in coord.Config, validated at Open, no silent defaults: HoldTTLDefault, HoldTTLMax, MaxHoldsPerClaim, MaxSubscribers, MaxTaskFiles, OperationTimeout, HeartbeatInterval, NATSReconnectWait, NATSMaxReconnects. Zero-deps posture: stdlib + nats.go + go-libfossil + EdgeSync only; any addition requires an ADR. CI enforces: gofmt, go vet, staticcheck, errcheck, go test -race, line-length <= 100, function-length <= 70, no TODOs in non-test code.

Phase 1 invariants (TigerStyle, 2026-04-18) — asserted in every coord public method: (1) ctx != nil; (2) TaskID non-empty and matches ID shape; (3) agent_id non-empty; (4) files non-empty, len <= MaxHoldsPerClaim, paths absolute, sorted before use; (5) HoldTTL > 0 and <= MaxHoldTTL; (6) Claim is atomic — partial hold acquisition is rolled back before error return; (7) Release closure is idempotent; (8) Coord cannot be used before Open or after Close; (9) Config.Validate runs at Open, panics on invalid; (10) all public methods return (_, error), no swallowed errors. Panics reserved for programmer errors; sentinel errors (ErrHeldByAnother, ErrClaimTimeout, ErrTaskNotFound, ErrAgentMismatch) for operating errors.

ADR 0007 (2026-04-19) flipped coord.Claim: signature is now Claim(ctx, taskID, ttl) — files dropped, read from task record. Order is task-CAS first then holds. Release un-claims the task in addition to releasing holds (otherwise tasks leak on crash between release and CloseTask).

Phase 2 moved tasks from fossil-files to NATS JetStream KV (decided 2026-04-19). Fossil is now for code artifacts only; enters in Phase 5+. ADR 0004's task-state language narrowed to code artifacts by ADR 0006. Compaction of closed tasks into repo ADRs is Phase 4 roadmap.

coord.Claim fires its task-CAS check before the file-hold check, so a concurrent second claim against the same task returns coord.ErrTaskAlreadyClaimed (not ErrHeldByAnother). Confirmed by coord/claim_test.go:142. examples/two-agents Step 2 asserts the CAS sentinel.

Phase 1 architectural direction (Ousterhout review, 2026-04-18): (1) coord is the sole exported package; internal/holds, internal/tasks, internal/chat are unexported. (2) Scoped holds via closure/return-release, not announce/release pairs. (3) NATS subjects and fossil txn refs NEVER appear in coord public signatures. (4) Conflict resolution = fossil fork + chat notify, not configurable. Closes README Open Questions #3 and #4. ADRs 0001-0004 pending (see agent-infra issue for tracking).

Cross-repo review agent claimed Invariant 3 (AgentID non-empty) was unenforced. Spot-check found coord/config.go:80-81 already has 'if c.AgentID == "" { return fmt.Errorf... }' in Config.Validate. Agents can be confidently wrong about 'X is missing / not enforced / not covered' claims — they scan, and scans miss things. Rule: before acting on any agent finding of the form 'X is not enforced / not present / not covered', grep the specific invariant or field name first.

coord.SubscribePattern is a raw NATS substrate leak: pattern matches the hashed 8-char SHA-256 ThreadShort segment, NOT the thread-name string passed to Post. So 'room.*' cannot match anything; use '*' or a literal observed ThreadShort. Name-prefix matching is deferred (ADR 0009 option 3 / ticket agent-infra-6wv). See examples/two-agents stepWildcard.

Orchestration pattern for parallel agent dispatch (Phase 2 wave-2, 2026-04-19): before fanning out agents to write leaf files, first land the plumbing commits inline — struct wiring (e.g. tasks.Manager field on Coord), stub deletion, shared accessor types (Task accessors on types.go). With the shared surface already committed, three agents (zib/udn/c46) worked open_task.go, ready.go, close_task.go concurrently with zero merge conflicts. Rule: pre-commits absorb the shared surface, parallel agents touch only leaf files.

Phase 3 orchestration (2026-04-19): sequential-with-verify beat parallel here. j6k → is8 → mra → 2w5 each ran serially because every ticket edited coord/coord.go (Post body, Ask body + Answer append, Subscribe + struct field + Close wiring). Parallel dispatch would have produced merge conflicts on that single file. The rule: parallel is right when leaf files are disjoint (Phase 2 wave-2 pattern); sequential is right when a single orchestration file absorbs every iteration. The verify step between each sequential agent caught a real bug (Phase 3B notify thread-cache miss) before it contaminated downstream work.
